name: Bender Fuzz
explanation: A thick, gated germanium fuzz in the vintage three-transistor lineage. Three cascaded asymmetric germanium-style clipping stages build a hard, mid-forward wall of fuzz that is grittier and more aggressive than a typical Fuzz Face or Big Muff. An envelope-tracking gate chokes the output as notes decay, giving the ragged staccato cut-off and 'spit' the circuit is famous for. A tilt Tone control shapes brightness and Level sets output. Controls: Fuzz, Gate, Tone, Level.
